title: California city mayor admits acting as illegal agent for China; how she ‘silently operated’
description: Arcadia Mayor Eileen Wang has agreed to plead guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government. She is accused of promoting pro-PRC propaganda and sharing favorable articles without notifying the US government. Wang's attorneys stated she accepts responsibility for past personal mistakes and apologizes to the community.

Gurgaon shocker: Cousin strangles woman to death, hides body in bed box
description: A 23-year-old woman was allegedly strangled to death by her maternal cousin in IMT Manesar, Gurgaon. The accused, who was seeking work, reportedly hid her body in a bed box. The murder was discovered by a colleague when the victim failed to report for work.

Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
description: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: A US family sold their company for $1.7 bn and gave 540 workers $240 mn of it
description: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Monica Seles and Tom Golisano’s massive fortune revealed after marriage news
description: Tennis legend Monica Seles and self-made billionaire Tom Golisano share a life built on success and quiet strength, with a combined net worth nearing $7 billion. The couple met around 2009 and married privately, now living a low-key life in Florida, supporting charities and exploring Monica Seles' recent health diagnosis.
